post.description:
Gets the full details of a booking.

* If the system cannot find a matching booking, then it will be returned as **null**, while `Success` status only indicates that there were no errors while processing the request.
* The latest **collected** and **refunded** status of order lines should always be returned via this endpoint (either by a call to cinema or the `Booking Stream` feature providing real-time updates of Connect booking data).
* The **booking print stream** is only returned if the `IncludeBookingPrintStream` is specified.
* The `ReturnTransactionStatusInfoIfSingleBookingMatch` can be used to refresh the full booking details from cinema. This is useful if items may have been added/updated at cinema.
* It is recommended to make in the context of the original purchasing loyalty member (using the `loyaltySessionToken` header). In this case, the signed-in loyalty member's id will be used, and the `MemberId` passed in on the request will be ignored.
* If a member is not logged in, then an HttpForbidden response will be returned with error code 34 (NoLoyaltyMember). Requesting a booking with `MemberId` or no member context is possible but requires specific security behaviour configuration to be enabled.
* Error Code 61 will be returned if the order is not uniquely identified through userSessionId

#### Valid Identifier Combinations

This endpoint can find a booking using different identifier combinations.

Valid options include:
* CinemaId and BookingId
* CinemaId and BookingNumber
* CinemaId and VistaTransNumber
* CinemaId and Barcode
* UserSessionId(no cinema required)

Sets the status of a booking to cancelled at the cinema level. Only unpaid bookings can be cancelled, paid bookings must be refunded.

#### Error codes
- **47**: Booking does not exist
- **48**: Booking is not an unpaid booking. Paid bookings must be refunded
- **49**: Booking has already been cancelled
- **20000**: Booking is being edited

Sets booking items as collected at the cinema through Sales Server.This can be called to mark items as collected after payment if tickets have been printed.

**PrepareConcessionsOnly** can be set to true to only mark the concessions as collected, if the **PreparationRequiresBookingCollection** system setting is set to 'Yes' at the cinema. If this is specified, a** PickupNumber** will be returned along with a **PickupAreas** list, with the concessions that can be picked up in each area. Any concessions that do not have a pickup area are not included.

Refunds a booking.Error codes below are returned in the response body

1. **100** - The booking has already been refunded
2. **110** - An item In the booking has already been refunded
3. **120** - The selected booking has already been picked up
4. **130** - The Selected booking contains one or more sessions where the cut-off time for refunds has past for this sales channel
5. **140** - To prevent fraud, there is a five minute delay before you can perform a refund after resending a confirmation email

post.description:
Search for bookings.

* When searching for multiple bookings, you must either provide both a session date and card info, or a MemberId.
* The **collected** and **refunded** status of order lines will only be returned by this endpoint if the `Booking Stream` feature is enabled allowing real-time updates of Connect booking data.
* It is recommended to make in the context of the original purchasing loyalty member (using the `loyaltySessionToken` header). In this case, the signed-in loyalty member's id will be used, and the `MemberId` passed in on the request will be ignored.
* If a member is not logged in, then an HttpForbidden response will be returned with error code 34 (NoLoyaltyMember). Requesting a booking with `MemberId` or no member context is possible but requires specific security behaviour configuration to be enabled.

get.description:
Retrieve the payment status on an unpaid booking ONLY when using the redirect-payment method.
When using the mark-paid method, Vista does not know the status of the payment so this is irrelevant (payment is made in external system).
This token is returned by a call to start an external payment for an unpaid booking. i.e.: /booking/unpaid/start-redirect-payment
The booking details token is automatically generated by the system to ensure that it is unique per payment.

post.description:
Marks an unpaid booking as paid.
1. Payment is assumed to have been made externally to Vista.
2. Payment details provided are recorded at cinema for auditing/reporting purposes.

#### Notes on Refunding Payments
1. Payments can be refunded as a** matched refund payment** as part of the** V2/Bookings/RefundBooking** end-point.
2. **BankTransNumber**, ** ReversalData** and** RefundPaymentMethod** must be specified to enable matched refund payment.

#### Error codes
- **47** - Booking does not exist
- **48** - Booking is not unpaid
- **49** - Booking has been cancelled
- **69** - Amount paid does not match booking value
- **20000** - Booking is being edited
- **20100** - Booking contains unvalidated voucher tickets

Start payment for an unpaid booking.

#### Error codes
- **47** - Booking does not exist
- **48** - Booking is not unpaid
- **49** - Booking has been cancelled
- **69** - Amount paid does not match booking value
- **20000** - Booking is being edited
- **20100** - Booking contains unvalidated voucher tickets
Update the comments or hold flag on an unpaid booking.

By default, returns cached value of the Member. Set ForceCacheRefresh = True to force a reload.
Activates a loyalty member associated with the [MemberId].

Send Loyalty member data to be created after a successful payment. A loyalty membership activation concession item needs to be included in the order. This endpoint is designed for purchasing a loyalty membership
### Before calling this endpoint
This endpoint is used as part of a loyalty membership purchase flow. Before calling this endpoint, a loyalty membership activation concession item needs to be included in the order with the **IsLoyaltyMembershipActivation** flag set to true.
### Creating members
For members to be created successfully, we also recommend setting the MemberItemId property in the request with the ActivationItemId of a selected member program from /Odata.svc/MembershipPrograms.

Completes the password reset process and updates the loyalty member's password.

#### Initiating a Password Reset
Use** InitiatePasswordReset** to generate a password reset code.

#### Validating a Password Reset Code
Use **ValidatePasswordResetCode** to check whether a reset code is still active (optional).

Even if the reset code is valid, it is possible that the password was unable to be updated due to rules configured in loyalty.
In this case IsResetCodeValid will be true but an error code will be included

/// #### Key Password Error Codes
* 33000    - Password does not meet the maximum length requirement.
* 33001    - Password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
* 33002    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of numeric characters.
* 33003    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of non-numeric characters.
* 33004    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of distinct characters.
* 33005    - Password cannot contain the user's email address.
* 33006    - Password cannot contain the username.
* 33007    - Password cannot contain the user's first or last name.
* 33008    - Password is a known compromised password.
* 33009    - Password contains disallowed words.
* 33010    - Password update failed at Loyalty.

Updates a member's details. Most of the validation rules around what parts of a member can and cannot be updated are defined in the Loyalty system itself so knowledge of the Loyalty configuration is recommended before using this API. Connect returns validation errors from Loyalty as a single **Membership Error** result code with the **ErrorDescription** providing more information. The **MembershipErrorCode** property can be used for programmatic handling of specific Loyalty validation scenarios.
#### Key Result Codes
* 0     - OK
* 1     - Unexpected error
* 3     - Membership error

#### Key Membership Error Codes
* 4    - The External ID entered already exists for another membership
* 21    - The Email Address entered already exists for another membership
* 30    - Mobile phone number is already in use
* 51    - The National ID entered already exists for another membership
* 42    - Updating Pin number is not allowed
* 70    - Mobile Phone is required
* 71    - Home Phone is required
* 72    - Mobile Phone cannot be updated via Connect API
* 72    - Home Phone cannot be updated be updated via Connect API

#### Key Password Error Codes
* 33000    - Password does not meet the maximum length requirement.
* 33001    - Password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
* 33002    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of numeric characters.
* 33003    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of non-numeric characters.
* 33004    - Password does not contain the required minimum number of distinct characters.
* 33005    - Password cannot contain the user's email address.
* 33006    - Password cannot contain the username.
* 33007    - Password cannot contain the user's first or last name.
* 33008    - Password is a known compromised password.
* 33009    - Password contains disallowed words.

post.description:
Validate member verifies the users credentials with the loyalty service.
If the member is valid the member is automatically associated with the UserSessionId
and the LoyaltyMember data is returned in the response.

The client permission 'AllowLoyaltyValidateWithoutPassword' controls which member details need to be specified.

When this is not linked to the current client profile, members can only be validated based on a login and password, or email and password, combination.
When this is linked to the current client profile, members may, in addition, be validated by either their ID or card number.

#### Result Codes
* 0     - OK
* 1     - Unexpected error
* 4     - Invalid request
* 17    - Card not found. This is primarily returned when incorrect credentials are provided when logging in with a loyalty card number
* 18    - The provided credentials were invalid
* 19    - Loyalty timeout

#### Extended Result Codes
* 30100 - The supplied card has an invalid status for login. This may be because the card is inactive, has expired or has been revoked/reported as stolen
* 30101 - The supplied credentials are for a membership to a club that is not accessible via the current sales channel
* 30102 - The member is temporarily locked after exceeding the login attempt limit (Note: The response property LockOutPeriodInSeconds will be populated as well)

Returns a queryable list of scheduled films that are currently showing. This is determined by DaysAheadToFlagSessionsAsNowShowing configuration.
Example: setting DaysAheadToFlagSessionsAsNowShowing to 4 will show all films that have scheduled sessions by the end of 4th day in the future.
See the 'Scheduled Films' documentation for more details.
- Setting the `includeAdvanceSales` parameter to false will exclude all films that have an opening date in the future.
- Expand options: Cast, Sessions
- The returned scheduled films (and expanded sessions) are filtered by sales channel which comes from either the `salesChannel` parameter provided, the `connectApiToken` header or the default sales channel defined in the Connect configuration.

get.description:
Returns a list of tickets for a specified session.

Can optionally filter on sales channel, and optionally include loyalty tickets(linked to the user session id via the ValidateMember call)

The maximum number of tickets per order is available in the `QuantityAvailablePerOrder` property of the response:
* For non-recognition tickets, this is defined by the `DefaultMaxTicketsPerOrder` system setting
* For recognition tickets this is defined when setting up the loyalty recognition limit
* For dynamically priced tickets this is calculated based on the price adjustment rule
* The quantity available per order will be the lowest value between the Loyalty recognition limit and the default setting

This endpoint is designed to be called as part of a UI ticketing process and by default will only return tickets appropriate for the current session and current user. However the following flags can provide more generic results:
* `includeLoyaltyTicketsForNonMembers` will return tickets marked as **loyalty only** for requests that are not made under a loyalty session
* `includeUnearnedLoyaltyRecognitionTickets` will return tickets only available when member has a matching **loyalty recognition** even if the caller has not earned it. This can be combined with `includeLoyaltyTicketsForNonMembers` to return all loyalty-related tickets regardless of caller.
* `includeCompTicketsForNonCompSession` will return complimentary tickets even if specified session is marked as non-complimentary
*  The **V2.BatchData** endpoint for cinema data will return all tickets and is designed for clients who wish to filer tickets in the UI themselves

#### Third-party membership ticket
Third-party membership tickets will only be returned when the cinema you're querying is configured to allow paid bookings only.

#### ExtendedResult Codes
1. 4 = Invalid request data
2. 50 = Session could not be found

Returns a list of tickets for a specified barcode.

This operation is allowed on the public profile by default.

See **GetTicketsForSession** for the information on behaviour and parameter usage.

##### Expected Response Codes

* 4 = Invalid request data
* 50 = Session could not be found

##### Expected ExtendedResponse Codes

Returns concession items configured for the specified cinema. Concessions are loaded from cinema based on the concession profiles linked to Connect clients configured at cinema. Concessions can additionally be linked to cinema operators in Connect directly.

#### Loyalty Behaviour
- Concessions can be flagged as Loyalty Only(available to all members) or Loyalty Recognition Only(available to members who have earned specific recognition)
- Unlike the GetTicketsForSession end-point loyalty concessions are returned for anonymous callers by default and clients expected to filter concessions appropriately
- If a **userSessionId** is not provided, all Loyalty Only and Loyalty Recognition Only concessions are returned
- If a **userSessionId** is provided, Loyalty Recognition Only concessions that have not been earned by the member are not returned

#### Filtering concessions by cinema operator
To limit what items are available for purchase by a patron to only the items at the cinema where they have their booking with, call this endpoint with **CinemaOperatorCode** specified.

Note that concession profile data must be loaded into Connect before this method of filtering can work. See the Connect setup docs for more information.

Returns the Order associated with the UserSessionId.
If the order doesn't exist, an empty order object will be returned but not persisted.
Completed orders should be fetched via the Bookings API.
Taxes and booking fees are calculated for the order and returned in the response Order.

Cancels the order associated with the UserSessionId.
* Associated cinema order will also be cancelled and any reserved seats will be released as will any other associated resources including 3rd Party Member tickets.
* Orders may also be cancelled by cleanup-tasks in the background so attempting to cancel an already cancelled order will result in a successful result (the `OrderNotFound` response property can be used to check for this scenario).
* Loyalty sessions associated with the UserSessionId will NOT be disconnected, subsequent calls re-using the UserSessionID will retain the member's context.

<para>If ReturnOrder is true, taxes and booking fees are calculated and returned in the Order response.</para>

#### In-seat delivery
DeliveryOption must be set to 1 for in-seat delivery.

For package items, DeliveryOption must be set on the package parent item. However, DeliveryInfo must
be specified on the items rather than the parent item.

DeliveryWindowValue can be set either at the order-level (affecting all concessions with in-seat delivery) or at
the item-level per concession. An item-level setting will override any order-level setting.

#### Adding a Loyalty membership activation
When adding a Loyalty membership activation item to an order, make sure to set the **isLoyaltyMembershipActivation** flag to
true. This must be done in order for the membership to be activated successfully when calling the **SetMemberToActivateOnComplete** endpoint.

#### Key Response Codes
* 0     - Concessions were successfully added
* 4     - Invalid request data - FailedConcessions collection may be inspected for details

#### FailedConcession Error Codes
* 164   - Concession exceeded selling limit (a.k.a. sold out/stock unavailable)
* 207   - Concession not currently available for sale due to selling time restrictions

post.description:
The final step in making a Connect order.

##### Steps Performed by CompleteOrder
1. Generates Vista Booking Number
2. Performs/records order payments
3. Commits transaction in VISTAIT and at the cinema.
4. Sends order confirmation notifications to customer.

##### Payment Processing Notes
1. For the order to be committed payments must be recorded matching the order value.
2. The** PaymentInfo** and** PaymentInfoCollection** properties are used to provide payment information.
3. The** PerformPayment** property indicates whether the payments are to be processed by Vista or merely recorded. Set this flag to false if payment has been already processed by an external system.
4. Each** PaymentInfo** should specify either the value of the payment or use the** BillFullOutstandingAmount** flag to pay the outstanding balance at the time of processing.
5. If** BillFullOutstandingAmount** is set for **partial gift card payment**, then the **PaymentTenderCategory** must be appropriately set to one of SVS, STORED VAL, STOREDVALUE, STOREDVALUESYSTEMS or SVC
6. The order payments are processed can be configured in Connect.If configuration has not been set then payments are processed in the order they appear in the** PaymentInfoCollection**.

##### Booking Modes
1. 0 = Paid Booking
2. 1 = Unpaid booking(pay on pick-up)
3. 2 = Zero value booking(no payment required)
4. 3 = Confirmed Unpaid booking(pay on pick-up, order will not get auto-released)

#### Notes on Refunding Payments
1. Payments can be refunded as a** matched refund payment** as part of the** V2/Bookings/RefundBooking** end-point.
2. For payments processed by Vista(either through this end-point or as part of the** Ticketing/StartExternalPayment Web Payment Module** process) payment data will be recorded appropriately to support a matched refund
3. For payments made externally(PerformPayment = false on this end-point) the** BankTransactionNumber**, **ReversalData** and **RefundPaymentMethod** must be specified on the **PaymetnInfo**

#### Response Codes
- **0**     - OK
- **1**     - Unexpected error
- **4**     - Invalid request data
- **12**    - Payment was declined by payment provider
- **14**    - An unexpected error occurred after payment was taken, one or more payments HAVE NOT been voided
- **25**    - An unexpected error occurred after payment was taken, all payments HAVE been voided
- **40**    - Incorrect booking mode
- **62**    - Order does not exist
- **66**    - Complete order without performing payment disallowed
- **69**    - Payment total does not equal order total value
- **83**    - Order is already being completed
- **10300** - Can not pay with gift card that is being recharged
- **10301** - Can not purchase gift card recharge in unpaid booking
- **10302** - Voucher tickets not validated
- **10303** - Invalid card supplied for card promotion
- **10304** - Order with card promotion is not paid
- **10305** - Card promotions do not support card wallet payments
- **10306** - Invalid optional member ID
- **10307** - Can not perform loyalty points payment without logged in loyalty member
- **10308** - Can not perform loyalty points payment with another member's points
- **10309** - Exceeded loyalty points payable value for order
- **10310** - Exceeded loyalty member points balance
- **10313** - Loyalty Points balance is unavailable
- **10314** - Can not complete booking edit orders with this endpoint. Use the Complete Edit endpoint instead
- **10315** - Complete order for Unpaid Booking disallowed
- **11100** - Payment voucher/pin is invalid
- **11101** - Payment voucher is already redeemed
- **11102** - Payment voucher is invalid and cannot be redeemed
- **11103** - Payment voucher is expired
- **11105** - Payment voucher configuration error
Allows payment to be made on an Order separately from the CompleteOrder call. Does not support split payments.

- Payment vouchers cards can be removed via the **RemovePaymentVoucher** end-point.
- Order details cannot be modified after payments have been applied to prevent inconsistencies during order completion.

#### Response Codes

- **11100** - Payment voucher/pin is invalid
- **11101** - Payment voucher is already redeemed
- **11102** - Payment voucher is invalid and cannot be redeemed
- **11103** - Payment voucher is expired

Queries an order to check that it has a corresponding booking in Vista Cinema, and will also mark an order as complete if the booking was successfully created in Cinema.This method can be called when order completion fails(for example, due to a timeout) to confirm whether the booking was created in Cinema or not.

When this method is called against an expired order, it will always return the 10200 code (OrderExpired), regardless of the booking's status in Cinema. The order will then be cancelled through the clean-up task.

##### Response Codes
* 0     - Booking exists at cinema
* 1     - Unexpected error
* 4     - Invalid request data
* 47    - Booking does not exist at cinema
* 61    - Order cannot be uniquely identified
* 62    - Order does not exist
* 63    - Order has been refunded
* 10200 - Order expired")

Adds a set of Season Pass tickets to the order and immediately attempts to reserve these tickets and seats at the cinema.
Season Pass tickets can be only be sold to Event screenings that have Season Passes available for sale.
If promotional pricing (season pass deal pricing) has been set up for any of the season pass tickets, the price will be adjusted accordingly and can be inspected by retrieving the order(setting ReturnOrder to true in the request will return the order and the prices of the individual tickets added).

##### Extended Result Codes
1. 4 = Invalid request data
2. 50 = Session could not be found

Attempts to set the seats for tickets on the order for the specified session.If seats can be successfully reserved they will replace any existing seat selections.The number of seats requested must match the number of seats required by the session's tickets. Seat numbering information can be returned from the Ticketing/AddTickets, Ticketing/GetSessionSeatData or the Data/GetSessionSeatPlan end-points.

#### Expected Response Codes
1. **8** - One or more of the specified seats could not be reserved

Returns screen layout and seat availability data for a specified session in an order.

* If session does not feature seat allocation no data is returned.
* Tickets are required to have been added to the order for this session before this end-point can be called.
* Seats that belong to this order are marked with status 4.
* `SeatDataFormat` should be set to *2* for performance reasons unless the legacy seat format is required

Initiates the payment process for the order for external payment scenarios including custom external payments or Vista** Web Payment Module** payments.

##### Web Payment Module Scenario
1. When** AutoCompleteOrder** is set to True Connect will perform the payment redirection via the appropriate payment connector.
2. The order will be completed automatically on successful completion of the payment.
3. Clients should poll the **GetOrderDetails** end-point for the outcome of the payment and the order completion steps.

##### Custom External Payment Scenario
1. When** AutoCompleteOrder** is set to False it is assumed that the client will perform payment externally to Vista.
2. Once payment is complete externally the client should call **CompleteOrder** to record the payment and commit the transaction.

##### Combining Gift Cards with External/Redirect Payments
1. Use** ApplyGiftCard** to add gift cards to the order before calling** ExternalPaymentStarting**.
2. External / **Web Payment Module** payment payment should be for the balance (Order Total - Gift Card Total)
2. Applied gift cards will be processed as payment as part of the** CompleteOrder** process either initiated by the client directly or by** Web Payment Module** automatically.

##### Restrictions After Payment Start
1. Once** ExternalPaymentStarting** is called the order cannot be modified.
2. The order can still be cancelled however until the order is complete - note that any completed payments must be voided/refunded by the client.

Adds a set of tickets to the order and immediately attempts to reserve these tickets and seats at the cinema.
* If `ReturnSeatData` is true the GetSessionSeatData command is called and the response data returned.
* If `ReturnOrder` is true taxes and booking fees are calculated and returned in the Order response.
* `ReturnSeatDataFormat` should be set to *2* for performance reasons unless the legacy seat format is required.

#### Seat Allocation
For allocated sessions seats will be auto-allocated unless `SkipAutoAllocation` is provided or `SelectedSeats` are provided.
If allocation cannot be made automatically but there are still enough seats are available for manual selection then one of two things will happen:
* If `UserSelectedSeatingSupported` is true then tickets will be added (response code 0) but seats not reserved.Check the `Order` tickets to ensure seat allocation was successful.
* If `UserSelectedSeatingSupported` is false then the tickets will not be booked (response code 1) and**31** extended result code will be returned

#### Key Response Codes
* 0     - Tickets were added, seats may or may not have been allocated.
* 1     - Tickets were not added, see extended result code and error description for more details.
* 4     - Invalid request data.
* 8     - Tickets could not be added as either there were not enough seats left in session or selected seats are already reserved.

#### Key Extended Result Codes
* 31    - The selected seats could not be automatically allocated by cinema auto-allocation process.
* 59    - Third party member ticket was not approved by third party.
* 10700 - An added card promotion ticket clashes with another card promotion ticket added to the order.
* 10701 - Logged in Loyalty member does not have enough redemptions for a provided recognition.
* 10704 - Voucher is invalid for the film.

IsRecurring.description: Gets a value indicating whether the member makes recurring or one-off payments for their subscription.

ReactivationItem.description: Gets the billing item to reactivate a {WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.LoyaltyMemberSubscriptionStatus.Frozen} subscription.

Gets a value indicating whether the member uses third parties to pay for their subscription.

Gets the date And time the member's next subscription payment is due.
Represents the end of the current billing period And start of the next billing period.

BillingError.description: Gets the billing error for the latest billing attempt if it failed.

A hold period allows a patron to pause their subscription for a period of time.

EndDate.description: Gets the date the hold period ends. The hold period is inclusive of this date.

Price.description: Gets the price of the item.

WillBeRetried.description:
Gets a value indicating whether another billing attempt will be made.
i.e. hasn't reached the configured maximum billing attempts.

UserSessionId.description: Order Identifier.  Unique 32 character code.

ForceCacheRefresh.description:
Whether the member returned should be from the cache if a value exists.
Default is to use the cache value.
Set to True to force a reload of the Member.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.GetMem.description:
Returns a list or set of lists of LoyaltyItems which are valid at the current date and time.
Many rewards or recognitions are only valid on specific dates, days or times of day.
Specifying a SelectedSessionDateTime allows the system to filter ticket types to the selected Session date and time.

UserSessionId.description:
The UserSessionId is the 32 chr unique identifier for the current customer order.
It also links the logged on Member with thier associated TicketingService.Order
This value MUST always be populated, as all loyalty member data is also persisted and retrieved using this key.

SelectedSessionDateTime.description:
If the user has purchased tickets or is browsing a specific session
the showing date and time should be passed here - which limits the tickets available to those valid for that time only.

GetTicketTypes.description: If true a list of Ticket Types which are available to the Member is returned

GetDiscounts.description: If true a list of general recognitions which are available to the Member is returned

GetConcessions.description: If true a list of Concessions which are available to the Member is returned

SupressSelectedSessionDateTimeFilter.description: If true the list of Ticket Types will not be filtered by the SelectedSessionDateTime parameter.

GetAdvanceBookings.description: If true a list of advance booking offerings available to the member are returned.

GetAdvanceSeatingRecognitions.description:
If true a list of advance seating recognitions available to the member are returned.
Members who have an advanced seating recognition can purchase restricted seats based on an advanced seating rule.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Loyalt.description: LoyaltyItems are returned from the LoyaltyService.GetMemberItemList method

VistaID.description:
VistaID contains the concession or tickets Vista unique identifier
for example Item_ItemId/Price_strCode or Item_strMasterItemCode/TType_strHOCode (if Loyalty uses HO Codes)

RecognitionID.description: RecognitionID is the loyalty unique identifier for the reward item.  This must be passed to Sales Server when ordering the item

BalanceTypeID.description:
BalanceTypeID a loyalty recognition 'grouping' identifier  This must be passed to Sales Server when ordering the item
This identifier links back to a related BalanceType entry that should be found in the LoyaltyMember.BalanceList collection
            
If this link is resolved the client application is responsible for validating that as recognitions are redeemed in a transaction
the total value of points used for each BalanceType does not exceed the Points available.
            
If this link is NOT resolved (i.e. a recognition item is available to the member but which does NOT have a BalanceTypeID
or the BalanceTypeID does not resolve to a BalanceType in the LoyaltyMember.BalanceList collection)
the client application should NOT allow the redemption/use of the LoyaltyItem.

MessageText.description: Message Text contains the text only message displayed at POS along side the option in the loyalty screen

QtyAvailable.description: This is the absolute quantity available to this member.  This value is not modified internally as items are ordered.

QtyEarned.description: This is the quantity earned and available for use for the member. This ignores day limits to hold the total quantity at the members disposal.

QtyIsUnlimited.description: True if the recognition can be redeemed unlimited times. If true QtyToTake and QtyEarned will equal the per-order limit of the recognition.

DisplayItem.description: Restricts which items should be displayed to the customers.

IsManuallySelected.description: Indicates whether or not this item is a manually selected recognition.

SequenceNumber.description: The sequence number of the recognition. Used to determine the origin of a recognition once it's redeemed.

PricingStructure.description: Indicates any points or price cost this recognition would incur when redeemed

PriceToUse.description: Price To Use. Null if the recognition price type is not price to use.

PointsCost.description: Points Cost. Null if no points costs is applicable, or the points cost is used as a ratio of the item price.

PercentageOff.description: Percentage Off. Null if not applicable

DollarAmountOff.description: Dollar Amount Off. Null if not applicable

For some request messages only the MemberId and or Card Number is required to identify the Member.

transaction detail with a child collection of transaction line items and associated points ajustments (points earned and redeemed).

contains complete transaction detail with a child collection of transaction line items and associated points ajustments (points earned and redeemed).

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Loyalt.description:
A Loyalty Member Transaction. The Transaction Id and
Transaction Line Item number uniquely identify the Transaction.

VistaTransactionId.description: The vista transaction number as generated by the sales channel at the time of transaction.

CinemaId.description: The loyalty complex (cinema) id associated with the transaction.

BalanceTypeId.description: The Id of the balance type associated with the transaction.

BalanceTypeName.description: The name of the balance type associated with the transaction.

ManualNote.description: A note manually entered by a Loyalty administrator about this transaction.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Loyalt.description: A transaction made by a loyalty member.

BookingId.description:
BookingId is a difficult-to-guess alphanumeric code that also identifies the booking.
It is what should be displayed to customers to use to collect their booking.
This is guaranteed unique across cinemas.
Note that some old transactions may have this null

LoyaltyTransactionId.description: The unique identifier for the transaction as defined by loyalty.

VistaTransactionId.description: The vista transaction identifier supplied to loyalty by the sales channel at transaction time.

LineItems.description: Collection of line items associated with this transaction.

TransactionDate.description: The date and time that the transaction was made.

PointsAdjustments.description:
List of points adjustments associated with the transaction directly, rather than a line item.
Typically the result of a manual points adjustment.

TotalValue.description: The total value of the entire transaction.

TotalBoxOfficeValue.description: The total value of all box office items within the transaction.

TotalConcessionsValue.description: The total value of all concession items within the transaction.

IsRefund.description: True if the transaction is a refund.

IsPointsAdjustmentOnly.description: Returns true if the transaction contains no line items, and at least one points adjustment against this transaction.

TotalPointsRedeemedByBalanceType.description: List of the total points redeemed on each balance type for the entire transaction.

TotalPointsEarnedByBalanceType.description: List of the total points earned on each balance type for the entire transaction.

TotalPointsBalanceByBalanceType.description:
List of the total points balance following the transaction for each balance type.
This property is populated when running Loyalty v4.4 or higher.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Loyalt.description: An individual line item belonging to a parent transaction.

TransactionLineItemId.description: Uniquely identifies each line item within the transaction.

ItemName.description: The name of the item purchased (or refunded) for the transaction line.

ItemNameAlt.description: The alternate name of the item purchased (or refunded) for the transaction line.

MovieCode.description:
The vista movie code. This will either be a Cinema code or HO code dependent on how Loyalty item data is loaded.
Only populated when the transaction line item is box office.

MovieName.description:
The movie name.
Only populated when the transaction line item is box office.

MovieNameAlt.description:
The alternate movie name.
Only populated when the transaction line item is box office.

SessionDate.description:
The session date and time.
Only populated when the transaction line item is box office.

PointsAdjustments.description: Collection of points balance adjustments (points earned/redeemed) for this transaction line item.

BoxOfficeValue.description: The total box office value for the line item.

ConcessionsValue.description: The total concession value for the line item.

TotalPointsRedeemedByBalanceType.description: List of the total points redeemed on each balance type for the line item.

TotalPointsEarnedByBalanceType.description: List of the total points earned on each balance type for the line item.

IsBoxOfficeItem.description: True if the transaction line item is box office.

IsConcessionItem.description: True if the transaction line item is a concession.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Loyalt.description: Points earned and redeemed for an individual transaction line item.

PointsEarned.description: Total points earned for the adjustment.

PointsRedeemed.description: Total points redeemed for the adjustment.

BalanceTypeId.description: The member balance type id for the adjustment.

BalanceTypeNameAlt.description: The alternate member balance type name.

ManualNote.description: A note manually entered by a Loyalty administrator about this adjustment.

MemberLogin.description: Member credentials used to Login.  Can be omitted if Member Card Number is supplied.

MemberPassword.description: Member credentials used to Login.  Can be omitted if Member Card Number is supplied.

MemberCardNumber.description: Member Card Number. Required unless a Login and Password are not supplied.

MemberId.description: Unique Membership Identifier. Used in a number of Loyalty and Ticketing service calls

Customer Phone

This field is NOT required if the ValidateMember call has been made,
which associates a member with the Order and enables richer Loyalty functionality.

The customer tax name

If True the PrintStream will be requested from the Cinema and stored with the UserSessionId for later retrieval
Processing of Print documents is always processed indepenantly as it is not considered a critical processing step if the customer has been charged
Use DataServices.GetPrintStream to retrieve the Print Stream once the order is completed successfully.

This Print Template name is used to identify the Print Template to use on the Cinema server and must match a
Print Template folder on the cinema server.  If no value is passed the service can be configured with a default template name.

True means the payment was take and order was completed.
False means order not yet complete (but could be paid - inspect `IsPaymentCompleted`)

VistaBookingNumber.description: On successful completion the VistaBookingNumber will contain the Vista Booking Number which can be displayed to the user for reference

VistaBookingId.description: On successful completion the VistaBookingId will contain a cinema generated key which can be displayed to the user for reference in place of the booking number

VistaTransNumber.description: On successful completion, VistaTransNumber contains the transaction number for this order which can be used to obtain the booking details from history tables

HistoryID.description: On successful completion, HistoryID contains a reference for the order details stored in historical tables

PrintStream.description: This property is deprecated. PrintStreamCollection should be used instead

PrintStreamCollection.description: If the ReturnPrintStream flag is True in the CompleteOrderRequest message the PrintStreamCollection field will contain print data if it was successfully retrieved from the Cinema

PaymentInfoCollection.description: If handling multiple payments, PaymentInfoCollection is returned with payment data passed to Vista and updated with some processing information (payment status, payment connector error codes)

BalanceList.description: Contains the list of points balances for the Loyalty Member associated with the Order

PassCollection.description: Contains a collection of passes associated with the order

BackgroundJobUrl.description: If the order is being completed in the background, this url can be polled to check for the status of the background job

LoyaltyPointsCost.description: Specifies the Loyalty points cost of the order.

OrderEmails.description: The emails that Connect attempted to send and the result of each. An email appearing in this list as sent means that the email request was successfully sent to the configured SMTP server and does not guarantee the email reached the intended recipient. If an email was expected to be sent and either does not appear in this list or is accompanied with EmailSent false consult the logs to determine the cause of failure.

ParentBookingId.description: Id of the parent booking (advanced food and beverage).

JourneyReference.description: A reference to a Journey that will be created at cinema

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.OrderP.description:
After all tickets have been successfully added, seats allocated (either by the system or by the user),
Membership data collected, and payment details collected the Order can be completed.
This process will prepare the order for completion at the cinema,
if successful it will then perform payment services (if requested), and final commit the booking at the Cinema.

UserSessionId.description: Order Identifier.  Unique 32 character code.

PaymentInfo.description:
Contains the payment details for the order.
This information will be used to authorise payment with the Payment Services configured
in the VistaIT db.

CustomerZipCode.description:
Stores the zip code for the customer that creates the booking.
It is an optional field, however some banking systems require this to be set, the Exhibitor will instruct you if your application must supply this value.

UseAlternateLanguage.description:
Set if you want to generate your passes with alternate language data. i.e. The customer
is using the secondary language as defined in Vista.

CustomerLanguageTag.description: The language used by the customer of the application to process this order.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.AddSea.description: Used to add new tickets to an Order.  If the Order is not yet initialised it will automatically be loaded when this method is called.

UserSessionId.description: UserSessionId - identifies the current User order to add the tickets to.

CinemaId.description:
Each order can include Tickets for Sessions at a single Cinema at a time.
If the user wishes to change the Cinema after ordering tickets - existing tickets must be cancelled and a new Order created.

SeasonPasses.description: List of Season Passes to be added to the order.

ReturnOrder.description: Set to True to load and return the Order properties in the Response message.

UserSelectedSeatingSupported.description:
Set to True if the client application supports User Selected Seating.
If True, when the system fails to auto allocate the requested seats, the seats remain reserved
and the client application MUST ensure the customer allocates all seats manually.
Failure to allocate all seats manually will result in failure of the final order processing validation.

SkipAutoAllocation.description: Set to True if the client application supports User Selected Seating and requires that users always self select seats

BookingFeeOverride.description: Used to override the booking fee manually. Value is in cents.

BookingMode.description: This will affect how the booking fee is calculated if the Cinema Exhibitor has defined booking fees which are based on when the booking is paid or unpaid (0=Paid, 1=Unpaid, 2=ZeroValue, 3=ConfirmedUnpaid). Defaults to Paid.

ReturnDealSuggestions.description: Return suggested deals. Defaults to <see langword="true" /> when not specified.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Season.description: A ticket, or collection of tickets, allowing admission to multiple sessions that are part of an Event, typically at a lower cost than purchasing separate tickets for each session.

PromotionId.description:
Optional performance optimisation. When specified deal pricing is calculated solely for the specified deal. If not provided
all deals will be in scope for calculation.

SeasonPassTicketTypeId.description: Link to the SeasonPassTicketTypeId field of a Season Pass Deal Ticket Type

Qty.description: The number of Season Passes to order

Tickets.description: The list of tickets and related sessions that are being sold as a part of this Season Pass

SessionId.description: The Session Id this Season Pass Ticket is for

MaximumNumberOfSeatsPerRow.description: The largest number of contiguous seats available for the session

SelectedSeats.description: Seats to attempt assign to tickets

AreaCategoryCode.description: Area category of the area the selected seat is in

AreaNumber.description: Number of the area the selected seat is in

RowIndex.description: Zero-based row index of the selected seat

ColumnIndex.description: Zero-based column index of the selected seat

UserSessionId.description: The users session ID to associate the request to an order

TicketRemovals.description: The collection of Session Ids and related Ticket IDs which will be removed from the order. If this list is empty then all the tickets from all the sessions will be removed

ReturnOrder.description: Indicates whether the response should contain a serialised version of the updated order

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.AddTic.description: Used to add new tickets to an Order.  If the Order is not yet initialised it will automatically be loaded when this method is called.

UserSessionId.description: UserSessionId - identifies the current User order to add the tickets to.

CinemaId.description:
Each order can include Tickets for Sessions at a single Cinema at a time.
If the user wishes to change the Cinema after ordering tickets - existing tickets must be cancelled and a new Order created.

SessionId.description: The Session Identifier that the tickets contained with this request are for.

TicketTypes.description: The collection of TicketTypes which are being requested for this Session.

ReturnOrder.description: Set to True to load and return the Order properties in the Response message.

ReturnSeatData.description:
Set to True to include the load and return the SeatData for the Session in the Response message.
Only set to True for SeatAllocated Sessions, and if the client application supports User selected seating.
If false, the Id field of returned seats will be null. Defaults to false.

ProcessOrderValue.description:
This property is deprecated as of version 4.1.506. The order value will always be processed.
            
Set to True to include the Order Booking Fee and Tax calculations at the cinema before returning the Order
Ignored if ReturnOrder is not True

UserSelectedSeatingSupported.description:
Set to True if the client application supports User Selected Seating.
If True, when the system fails to auto allocate the requested seats, the seats remain reserved
and the client application MUST ensure the customer allocates all seats manually.
Failure to allocate all seats manually will result in failure of the final order processing validation.

SkipAutoAllocation.description:
Set to True if the client application supports User Selected Seating and requires that users always self select seats
Setting to True if UserSelectedSeatingSupported is False will lead to a incompletable order

IncludeAllSeatPriorities.description:
Set to True to include all available Seat Priority values in the SeatData.
Setting this value enables the client visibility of special seat statuses such as House and Special Seats, Broken Seats, Sofa seats etc.
If used - the client should display unrecognised statuses as equivalent to 'no seat' to accommodate future status values.
Ignored if ReturnSeatData is false.

IncludeSeatNumbers.description:
Set to True to include Seat Number details in the SeatData for the Session in the Response message.
Only set to True if the client application supports parsing and displaying Seat Number data.
Ignored if ReturnSeatData is false.

ExcludeAreasWithoutTickets.description:
If True Seating Areas which have no seats defined or available within the order are excluded from the seat map.
Ignored if ReturnSeatData is false.

ReturnDiscountInfo.description:
When this property is true the AddTicketsResponse order will include a list of available discounts on the tickets that were submitted as part of
the AddTicketsRequest. If set to true, this will override ReturnOrder and will return the order contents (including the Discount information) in the response.

BookingFeeOverride.description: Used to override the booking fee manually. Value is in cents.

BookingMode.description: This will affect how the booking fee is calculated if the Cinema Exhibitor has defined booking fees which are based on when the booking is paid or unpaid (0=Paid, 1=Unpaid, 2=ZeroValue, 3=ConfirmedUnpaid). Defaults to Paid.

ReorderSessionTickets.description:
Set True to re-order the tickets for the session specified by `SessionId` to get ensure all seats are grouped together if possible.
This will cancel and re-order all tickets resulting in re-allocation, as such this is not compatible with the `SelectedSeats` parameter.

MultiSessionTicketOrder.description: Used for ordering tickets to multiple sessions

CustomerLanguageTag.description: Indicates the language in which customer-friendly error messages should be returned, if translations are available.

ReturnSeatDataFormat.description: Indicates the format of seat data to be returned if `ReturnSeatData` is true(0=All, 1=PipeDelimeted, 2=Structured)

IncludeCompanionSeats.description: Set to True to include Companion Seat Status in the SeatData for the Session in the Response message.

TicketTypeCode.description: Vista Ticket Type code to purchase. This field is required for standard tickets however for vouchers (added using `OptionalBarcode`) it is optional and in fact may be overridden with the ticket type attached to the barcode.

Qty.description: Quantity of tickets of this type.

PriceInCents.description:
Required.
Value of Ticket Type (including taxes) in cents.

OptionalBarcode.description: If the ticket is being requested with a voucher barcode specify the barcode here. This barcode will be validated and the ticket type associated with the barcode will be used in place of any value specified in `TicketTypeCode`. `OptionalAreaCategoryCode` should also be provided if voucher barcode may be linked to multiple ticket types.

OptionalBarcodePin.description: The pin number associated to the voucher barcode.

BookingFeeOverride.description: When this property is set on one or more tickets then the default booking fee is overridden by the combination of the ticket amounts.

ThirdPartyMemberScheme.description: An optional third party member scheme to specify member ticket parameters

LoyaltyRecognitionId.description: Loyalty recognition id. Used when the recognition acts on an existing ticket type.

LoyaltyRecognitionSequence.description: Identifies the specific instance of a recognition to redeem if multiple instances of the same recognitions are available for the member

OptionalAreaCategoryCode.description: If the ticket is being requested with a voucher barcode, an Area Category should be specified. This is required if your barcodes can be linked to multiple ticket types.

WSVistaWebClient.ServiceContracts.Sessio.description: Used to add tickets for multiple sessions in a single call.

SessionId.description: The Session Identifier that the tickets contained with this object are for.

TicketTypes.description: The collection of TicketTypes which are being requested for this Session.

MemberCard.description: The card number that the tickets contained with this request are for.

MemberDateOfBirth.description: Optional. The date of birth of the member.

Order.description:
A representation of the state of the order after the tickets have been added. Will only contain a value if "ReturnOrder" is
set to "true" on the request object

SeatsNotAllocated.description: Deprecated: (Inspect Order instead). Indicates that tickets have been added to the order for allocated sessions that do not have seats assigned. Can be used to drive UIs to seat selection pages.

AvailableSeats.description: Stores the total number of available seats for the current session.

MaxSeatsPerRow.description: The largest number of contiguous seats available for the session

SeatLayoutData.description: A representation of the seat data.

SessionStatuses.description: Lists the number of seats available remaining per ordered session

RedemptionsRemainingForVouchers.description: Lists the redemptions remaining for each voucher ticket type in the order

indicating that the client application MUST force the user to self select
If the AddTicketsRequest.UserSelectedSeatingSupported flag is false this condition causes the seats to be cancelled internally
